title: Conference of the Birds: The Thirty Birds (Complete)
description: Attar's complete allegory: thousands of birds set out to find the Simorgh, their king. Only thirty survive the journey. They discover that 'si murgh' (thirty birds) IS the Simorgh — they themselves are the god they sought.
introduction: Attar's complete allegory: thousands of birds set out to find the Simorgh, their king. Only thirty survive the journey. They discover that 'si murgh' (thirty birds) IS the Simorgh — they themselves are the god they sought.

translation.text: They traveled through seven valleys: the Valley of the Quest, the Valley of Love, the Valley of Knowledge, the Valley of Detachment, the Valley of Unity, the Valley of Bewilderment, and the Valley of Poverty and Annihilation. Of the thousands who set out, only thirty arrived. They looked into the face of the Simorgh and saw — their own faces. The thirty birds (si murgh) were the Simorgh. They had been seeking themselves.
